text.title: Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) in CI/CD Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the DAST CI config. Each rule pairs a CI-checkable invariant + a known incident pattern.

rule.statement: DAST target URL MUST resolve to a staging or pre-prod environment. Production hostnames MUST NOT appear in any DAST CI configuration.
rule.rationale: DAST is active scanning — it sends malformed requests, probes endpoints, and may trigger destructive operations. Against production it can cause outages, trip rate-limiters, and violate compliance.

rule.statement: A scope file (.zap/rules.tsv or equivalent denylist) MUST exist and explicitly exclude third-party services, payment endpoints, email senders, and destructive operations.
rule.rationale: Out-of-scope scanning can trigger real-money transactions, send test emails, or delete data — regardless of the environment label.

rule.statement: Nuclei templates MUST be pinned to a tagged version (e.g. v9.7.0). Floating HEAD of the community template repo is forbidden.
rule.rationale: New templates may be noisy or incorrect before review; floating HEAD causes overnight CI noise.

rule.statement: Run cadence MUST include at least one scheduled scan (nightly OR weekly). Post-deploy gate alone misses environment drift.
rule.rationale: Scheduled cadence catches issues introduced by data migrations, dependency updates in staging, or third-party drift between deploys.

rule.statement: Findings MUST be uploaded as SARIF to GitHub Code Scanning (or equivalent). Plain logs in CI artefacts only are forbidden.
rule.rationale: SARIF in the Security tab is searchable, triage-able, and dedup-able across scans. Plain logs decay.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
